identified_property: severity of debt terms and creditor

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: You owe $50,000,000 to a violent cartel enforcer who is charging compounding daily interest at 25%, has placed a bounty on you as collateral, and demands full repayment within 30 days.

variations.label: low
variations.text: You owe $50,000,000 to a ruthless loan shark who is charging 20% annual interest, has already begun seizing your assets, and has made explicit threats against your family if you miss a single payment.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: You owe $50,000,000 to an aggressive collections agency that has filed suit against you, is garnishing your wages, and is charging 15% annual interest with compounding penalties for any late payments.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: You owe $50,000,000 to a commercial bank under a standard loan agreement with 5% fixed annual interest and a structured 20-year repayment schedule.

variations.label: high
variations.text: You owe $50,000,000 to a wealthy family member who charges no interest, requires no fixed repayment schedule, and has told you to pay it back whenever you comfortably can.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: You owe $50,000,000 to a charitable benefactor who charges no interest, has already forgiven 90% of the debt, and asks only that you repay the remaining $5,000,000 in small installments over your lifetime at your own pace.

quality_checks.reasoning: All tiers describe owing a large debt originating from a $50M obligation. The severity of terms (interest rate, repayment timeline, penalties) and the nature of the creditor (violent cartel to charitable benefactor) vary monotonically. Each tier is strictly more preferable than the one before it, with clear qualitative differences in creditor dangerousness, interest burden, and repayment flexibility.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because the creditor is the most forgiving (charitable benefactor), the terms are the mildest (no interest, 90% forgiven, lifetime repayment at own pace), making the debt minimally burdensome compared to all other tiers.
